body:
# Feature request

## Summary

This could also apply to the upcoming `NavigationMenu` component.

## Examples in other libraries

- Facebook settings panel
- YouTube settings panel

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dcbdebe7-8b11-4c6e-ba3f-cbd2395bd73a

## Motivation

Non-cascading submenus work better on narrow screens.

comments.body: Hello, does this have any progress? I need to implement something similar and would love to have some guidance. Thank you
We're trying to figure out how to do an "accordion style" submenu

I have somewhat of a concrete API in mind.

The idea is to keep authoring submenus exactly as today (`Menu.SubmenuRoot` / `Menu.SubmenuTrigger`) and add a single prop on `Menu.Root` that decides how *all* of *its* submenus behave:

```tsx
<Menu.Root submenuBehavior="drilldown"> {/* default: "flyout" (current behavior) */}
```

When `submenuBehavior="drilldown"` is present, a `Pane` manages what submenu is visible by assigning each submenu an ID, rendering the submenu with the matching active ID.  This architecture, however, raises the following questions:

- Given the independence over every menu's `submenuBehavior`, one menu can have a drill-down behaviour while one of its submenus can still use `flyout`. In that case, should submenu components still require a `Portal`, `Positioner`, and `Popup` for their own submenus, or should they just share the ones from their parent menu? I'm more inclined towards the latter, preferably with a warning for the user when they are still present. 
- Should pressing the `Esc` key on a drill-down submenu close it with its parent menu (cascade the effect until the first non-drill-down menu or until the whole menu closes), or should it just go back to the previous menu?
- Similar to the previous point, how should clicking a non-submenu item behave?
- Should another component like the `View` or `Popup` act as the pane or should we have a separate `Pane` component?
- What would be the ideal approach for handling radio submenus, specifically for when we want to display the selected value on the submenu's trigger?
- Should an explicit `Submenu.Back` component be introduced?
- I couldn't find any a11y standard for this specific shape, but would be happy to take recommendations/advice. 

I built a [quick prototype of this](https://github.com/genzzo/base-ui/blob/drilldown-submenu/docs/src/app/(private)/experiments/menu/drilldown.tsx). It doesn't 100% reflect the intended architecture, just a stepping stone towards it. Would love to have a maintainer's eye on it.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1127ee5f-5097-49e5-b9ee-33193efc6455

title: [menu] Support non-cascading/inline submenus
